line: - Kept exported merge summary type aligned with the richer report surface
- procedural-entry conflicts against canonical parent handles
- distinguishes manual pending conflicts vs auto-resolved conflicts
- emits per-conflict audit rows plus a summary audit row with related run IDs
- Added core regression covering representative merge conflict + audit path
- Updated CLI regression to assert new merge report fields remain stable
- I broadcast completion via Agent Mail.
- There was earlier unrelated daemon test/build noise in `crates/membrain-daemon/src/daemon.rs`, but final `cargo check --all-targets` is green.

GROVE_LESSONS: ["Canonical procedural handles must be derived via ProceduralStore::pattern_handle during merge planning and tests, otherwise parent/fork conflict matching is false-negative.","Working-state divergence is only a pending conflict under manual merge strategy; under auto strategies it is treated as directly mergeable state, so conflict counts differ by strategy."]

items: Canonical procedural handles must be derived via ProceduralStore::pattern_handle during merge planning and tests, otherwise parent/fork conflict matching is false-negative.
Working-state divergence is only a pending conflict under manual merge strategy; under auto strategies it is treated as directly mergeable state, so conflict counts differ by strategy.

line: GROVE_DECISIONS: ["Kept merge explicit and auditable by surfacing per-conflict items plus summary audit sequence IDs instead of silently folding everything into aggregate counts.","Scoped concrete merge resolution to governed procedural entries and working-state divergence without inventing broader durable-memory merge semantics that are not yet represented in BrainStore."]

items: Kept merge explicit and auditable by surfacing per-conflict items plus summary audit sequence IDs instead of silently folding everything into aggregate counts.
Scoped concrete merge resolution to governed procedural entries and working-state divergence without inventing broader durable-memory merge semantics that are not yet represented in BrainStore.

line: GROVE_WARNINGS: ["Only two file reservations were actually released by ID; several earlier reservation requests had conflicts with other agents, so some requested paths were never granted to this agent.","Focused tests passed and workspace check passed, but I did not broaden into daemon-specific merge tests because the task was completed via core+CLI surfaces and the repo had concurrent activity."]

items: Only two file reservations were actually released by ID; several earlier reservation requests had conflicts with other agents, so some requested paths were never granted to this agent.
Focused tests passed and workspace check passed, but I did not broaden into daemon-specific merge tests because the task was completed via core+CLI surfaces and the repo had concurrent activity.
